title: Best Robo-Advisors in Canada (2026) | Westmount Fundamentals
description: A data-driven breakdown of the best robo-advisors in Canada for 2026. Compare management fees, minimum investments, portfolio performance, and features.

acceptedAnswer.text: A robo-advisor is an automated investment platform that builds and manages a diversified portfolio of exchange-traded funds (ETFs) for you based on your risk tolerance and financial goals, typically charging lower fees than traditional mutual funds or financial advisors.

mainEntity.name: What are the typical fees for a Canadian robo-advisor?

acceptedAnswer.text: In Canada, robo-advisor management fees usually range from 0.20% to 0.50% of your total assets under management (AUM) per year. In addition to this management fee, you will also pay the Management Expense Ratios (MERs) of the underlying ETFs, which average around 0.10% to 0.20%.

mainEntity.name: Is my money safe with a Canadian robo-advisor?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, funds held with major Canadian robo-advisors are safe. The cash and securities in your accounts are held by custodian brokerages that are members of the Canadian Investor Protection Fund (CIPF), which protects your assets up to $1 million per account type in the unlikely event the firm goes insolvent.

mainEntity.name: Can I hold RRSPs and TFSAs in a robo-advisor account?

acceptedAnswer.text: Absolutely. Almost all Canadian robo-advisors support registered accounts, including Registered Retirement Savings Plans (RRSPs), Tax-Free Savings Accounts (TFSAs), First Home Savings Accounts (FHSAs), and Registered Education Savings Plans (RESPs).

mainEntity.name: How does Questwealth Portfolios differ from Wealthsimple?

acceptedAnswer.text: Questwealth Portfolios uses an active management approach, where their portfolio managers adjust the ETF weightings based on market conditions, and charges a lower management fee (0.25% or 0.20% for larger balances). Wealthsimple uses a strictly passive, rules-based indexing approach and charges slightly higher fees (0.50% or 0.40% for larger balances) but offers a more integrated financial ecosystem with no minimum investment.
